chunks.text: Aydın Buluç Aydın Buluç I am a Senior Scientist at the Lawrence Berkeley National Lab (Applied Mathematics and Computational Research Division) and an Adjunct Faculty of the EECS (CS Division) at UC Berkeley. At LBNL, I am part of the Performance and Algorithms group. At UC Berkeley, I am part of the SLICE lab. I am also the Director of Sparsitute , a DOE Mathematical Multifaceted Integrated Capability Center (MMICC) focusing on sparse computations. I work on high-performance graph analysis and libraries, parallel sparse matrix computations, communication-avoiding algorithms, with applications in machine learning and computational genomics. Up-to-date information about current projects, students, and postdocs can be found in my research team's website: PASSION Lab . Previously, I was an Alvarez Fellow . LBNL recently ran a workshop in celebration of 20th year of Alvarez fellowshop . My unusually shallow genealogy includes only three lineal ascendants. We ran a workshop in celebration of John R. Gilbert's scientific contributions . My recent (Fall 2025) external engagements are: Helping run the DMML70 workshop in celebration of Jim Demmel's career. Speaking at the Simons Institute workshop on managing parallelism . Helping organize SIAM NCC'25 . Organizing the Networks for Science workshop . Presenting this paper at SC'25. You can pronounce my first name like this . The "ı" in my first name is a lowercase dotless i ( {\i} in LaTeX, ı in HTML and U+0131 in UTF-8). You can contact me at
